description: Discrete symbolic objects (integers 0-N)
Objects have fixed, recoverable identity (hashable)
A 'state' exists at each interaction step
Full trajectory is retained across steps
States are ordered sequences (position matters)
Rules apply iteratively to their own outputs
Deterministic input->output mapping exists
States are vectors in R^n (continuous, not discrete)
States exist in a vector space (addition, scalar multiplication defined)
States can be compared to each other
A notion of 'similar' vs 'different' exists
A scalar distance between states is defined
Distance satisfies triangle inequality and positivity
States can be grouped into clusters
States can be assigned to discrete classes
A sequence of discrete steps exists
The sequence of past states is accessible
State space is continuous (not discrete)
A notion of 'nearby' states exists
For each state, nearby states can be identified
Thresholds for what counts as 'persistent'
Thresholds for what counts as 'returned'
Thresholds for what counts as 'self-referential'
An external entity measures and classifies states
An act of extracting information from states
Two things can be non-identical
Something exists (at least one state)
States can differ across time steps
TARGET: Two things can be non-identical (the concept itself)
TARGET: A connection between two states exists
